title: Solver card
description: One solver's market listing for one network. Path: solvers/<network>/<name>.json

id.description: Canonical asset identity: "btc" or a 68-hex-char AssetId.

decimals.description: Decimals of the atomic unit: atomic units per display unit = 10^decimals. Matches the asset registry metadata field.

type.description: The price feed response is JSON.

price_path.description: RFC 6901 JSON Pointer to the numeric feed value, e.g. /price or /bitcoin/usd. Empty string selects the whole JSON document.

amount.description: A canonical decimal-string amount of atomic units: unsigned, no leading zeros. "0" disables the side it bounds.

enabledAmount.description: An amount that is strictly positive: the side it bounds is enabled.

name.description: Unique within the network directory; must equal the filename (without .json).

discovery_pubkey.description: BIP340 x-only public key, lowercase hex.

sig.description: BIP340 Schnorr signature by discovery_pubkey over sha256(canonical_json).

pair.description: Display pair "<base-ticker>/<quote-ticker>"; must equal base_asset.ticker + "/" + quote_asset.ticker. Canonical identity lives in base_asset.id / quote_asset.id.

base_asset.description: Display metadata for the pair's base side.

quote_asset.description: Display metadata for the pair's quote side.

price_decimals.description: The feed value divided by 10^price_decimals is the price in quote-atomic-units per base-atomic-unit.

min_base_amount.description: Lower trade-size bound for the base side, in base atomic units. An enabled side has 1 <= min <= max; when the side is disabled (max = "0") min MUST be "0".

max_base_amount.description: Upper trade-size bound for the base side, in base atomic units. "0" disables the side: the solver does not pay out base and makers cannot receive it.

min_quote_amount.description: Lower trade-size bound for the quote side, in quote atomic units. An enabled side has 1 <= min <= max; when the side is disabled (max = "0") min MUST be "0".

max_quote_amount.description: Upper trade-size bound for the quote side, in quote atomic units. "0" disables the side: the solver does not pay out quote and makers cannot receive it.
